visual: In a horse stance, one hand traces a continuous spiraling circle in front of the body driven by the waist, the palm rotating smoothly throughout.
motion_description: The practitioner maintains a wide, stationary stance, shifting weight continuously between the right and left legs. The left hand rests at the waist while the right hand traces a slow, continuous vertical circle in front of the body. As the weight shifts to the left leg, the torso turns slightly left and the right hand descends from face height across the centerline to the abdomen, palm rotating upward. The weight then shifts back to the right leg as the torso turns to the right, sweeping the right arm outward and upward to its starting position.
